user.title: Set up Object Event Monitor
user.description: Choose which Home Assistant labels control monitoring, objects, and event categories. Use security_system for alarm panels and state_monitor for on/off states. The integration emits dedicated HA events for your automations.

data_description.monitoring_label: Add this label to every entity that Object Event Monitor should watch. Usually keep device_monitoring.
data_description.object_labels: Labels that mean an object or location. Each monitored entity must have exactly one of these labels. One per line: home, restaurant, cafe.
data_description.object_names: How object labels should appear in events. The left side must match Object labels. Use home=Home, restaurant=Restaurant. Entries can be separated by lines or commas.
data_description.category_labels: Labels that mean a problem type or event category. One per line: power, security, internet. The script.tg_<object>_<category> package route requires a category label.
data_description.category_names: How category labels should appear in events. The left side must match Category labels. Use power=Power, security=Security. Entries can be separated by lines or commas.
data_description.on_state_values: Entity states that should be treated as ON for state_monitor. One per line or separated by spaces/commas. Default: on.
data_description.off_state_values: Entity states that should be treated as OFF for state_monitor. One per line or separated by spaces/commas. Default: off.
data_description.monitoring_timeout: Fallback timeout used when an entity has no label such as timeout_20s, timeout_7m, or timeout_1h.
data_description.heartbeat_interval: Reserved for future heartbeat support. Set 0 to disable.
data_description.debug_logging: Enable additional debug logging for this integration.

error.invalid_timeout: Timeout must be a positive duration.
error.invalid_heartbeat_interval: Heartbeat interval must be 0 or a positive number.
error.invalid_monitoring_label: The monitoring label may contain only lowercase letters, numbers, and underscores.
error.invalid_category_label: Category labels may contain only lowercase letters, numbers, and underscores.
error.monitoring_category_overlap: The monitoring label cannot also be a category label.
error.monitoring_object_overlap: The monitoring label cannot also be an object label.
error.category_object_overlap: The same label cannot be both a category label and an object label.
error.object_labels_required: At least one object label is required.
error.invalid_object_label: Object labels may contain only lowercase letters, numbers, and underscores.
error.invalid_label_name_mapping: Names must use label=Name format, one entry per line or separated by commas.
error.invalid_label_name_label: A label in the name dictionary may contain only lowercase letters, numbers, and underscores.
error.unknown_object_name_label: Object names can only use labels from the Object labels field.
error.unknown_category_name_label: Category names can only use labels from the Category labels field.
error.reserved_object_label: Object labels cannot match the monitoring label or category labels.
error.on_state_values_required: At least one ON state value is required.
error.off_state_values_required: At least one OFF state value is required.
error.on_off_state_overlap: ON and OFF state values cannot overlap.

abort.single_instance_allowed: Object Event Monitor is already configured.
